findings.problem: After Victor's meeting, the chapter spends the plotter-room scene and the Tuesday-Thursday montage re-proving a point the reader already has: Nate cannot perform Dominic's job and Ray is covering. Those beats have texture, but they do not buy a new consequence before the Eastbridge handoff, so the middle of the chapter drifts into scene redundancy and makes the Friday file reveal feel like the first real state change after line 289.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the stretch centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:393, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:407 so it does one of two things: either compress the repeated incompetence material, or make one of those beats change the chapter state in a new way. Useful options: let Nate's mistake touch Eastbridge directly, make Ray materially revise his trust level, or have Victor alter Nate's responsibilities because of what he sees. Do not keep three separate beats whose only payload is 'Nate is bad at this.'
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, on a cold read of the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:393, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:407, each block does distinct dramatic work and at least one of them creates a new consequence beyond generic humiliation. Fail if the section still reads as multiple variations of the same incompetence proof before the Eastbridge file is opened.

findings.problem: The chapter's three major workplace exchanges reuse the same turn-shape: Nate is asked to demonstrate competence, guesses wrong, an authority figure supplies the right term or procedure, and the beat lands on a dry tag. Each instance works locally, but together they make the dialogue architecture feel generated rather than lived, especially because the Ray and Victor scenes are supposed to escalate different kinds of pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the exchange design at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:241, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:355 so at least one scene stops using the quiz-and-correction ladder. Options that would create fresher pressure: Nate bluffs successfully and pays for it later, Victor withholds the correction and lets the silence work, or Ray abandons joking and asks the personal question before the technical fix. Keep the character voices, but change the leverage pattern.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least one of the three revised exchanges no longer follows question or test -> wrong answer -> expert correction -> joke or tag, and the reader can point to a different leverage shift in that scene by reading the page. Fail if all three workplace conversations still run on the same instructional ladder.

findings.problem: The narration repeatedly lands beats with clipped stand-alone fragments ('Not a tasteful stack.' 'Tenant sheet.' 'Timeline.' 'Wife present.' 'Just printer dirt.'). Used this often, the rhythm starts to feel like shorthand rather than close-third thought, and it runs against the constitution's ban on clipped narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the fragment beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:429, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:451, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:461, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:481 into grammatically complete narration or fold them into the surrounding sentences. Preserve the punch, but stop relying on subjectless fragment lines as a default emphasis tool.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the targeted beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:429, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:451, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:461, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:481 are readable as complete narration or syntactically attached to adjacent sentences. Fail if they remain stand-alone clipped fragments.

findings.problem: Ray's scene punctuation repeats the same physical formula three times: he starts to leave, doubles back, and delivers one more line. The repetition is noticeable at chapter scale and makes the handoff beats feel mechanically staged rather than observed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the exit beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:413, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:519. Let one line land without a return, bury one in ongoing action, or move one warning earlier so Ray's scenes do not all end on the same leave-then-return motion.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if those three Ray beats now resolve in three visibly different ways when read on the page, and no more than two of them use the same walk-away-then-come-back structure. Fail if all three still depend on the same exit fake-out.

summary: Voice, tense, and continuity control are strong: Nate, Ray, and Victor all track the style bible cleanly, the chapter stays in past tense, and the Eastbridge reveal lands with real pressure. It still fails award, craft, dialogue, and prose at review because the middle of the chapter redundantly re-proves Nate's incompetence, the major workplace conversations reuse the same quiz-correction ladder, and the prose leans on clipped-fragment and walk-away-return formulas often enough to feel ticky rather than fully controlled.
